feedback.text: Hi, There has been recently a lot of talk about video game preservation, and how companies can shut down at any time a game you have purchased. This is a critical issue with consumer, in multiple levels, it's an unfair and practice as well as an unfair term of service. We pay for a game and if the publisher decides so, they can take that purchase away, no money back, nothing to show for. If the Digital Fairness Act could look into the European initiative Stop Destroying games, it would be great, this initiative hopefully will go through, but still, more eyes and brains on an issue is always a positive! We just want the to have an end of life plan for what we paid for, without it going to the ether. Now for my second issue about Digital Fairness, as of couple weeks ago, visa and/or master card pressured 2 online shops to remove games from the storefront. Sure some were questionnable in nature and maybe should not have been sold there, but this is a shop issue, nothing that should be able to be handled or enforced by pressure from monopoles (utility?) companies that handle our money and how we can spend it. A line was crossed and as a consumer I am concerned about more form of censorship coming from those big companies towards our ability to do what we please online as long as it is within the law.
